heading: Grants to support high-quality charter schools
label: 7221b Grants to support high-quality charter schools

children.text: a State educational agency;
a State charter school board;
a Governor of a State; or
a charter school support organization.

children.label: A
children.text: open and prepare for the operation of new charter schools;

children.label: B
children.text: open and prepare for the operation of replicated high-quality charter schools; or

children.label: C
children.text: expand high-quality charter schools; and

provide technical assistance to eligible applicants and authorized public chartering agencies in carrying out the activities described in paragraph (1), and work with authorized public chartering agencies in the State to improve authorizing quality, including developing capacity for, and conducting, fiscal oversight and auditing of charter schools.

children.heading: State entity uses of funds
children.label: c State entity uses of funds
A
children.text: use not less than 90 percent of the grant funds to award subgrants to eligible applicants, in accordance with the quality charter school program described in the State entity’s application pursuant to subsection (f), for the purposes described in subsection (b)(1);

children.label: B
children.text: reserve not less than 7 percent of such funds to carry out the activities described in subsection (b)(2); and

children.label: C
children.text: reserve not more than 3 percent of such funds for administrative costs, which may include technical assistance.

children.heading: Contracts and grants
children.label: 2 Contracts and grants
children.text: A State entity may use a grant received under this section to carry out the activities described in subsection (b)(2) directly or through grants, contracts, or cooperative agreements.

children.label: i
children.text: the use of weighted lotteries in favor of such students is not prohibited by State law, and such State law is consistent with laws described in ; andsection 7221i(2)(G) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: such weighted lotteries are not used for the purpose of creating schools exclusively to serve a particular subset of students.

children.heading: Students with special needs
children.label: B Students with special needs
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to prohibit schools from specializing in providing specific services for students with a demonstrated need for such services, such as students who need specialized instruction in reading, spelling, or writing.

children.heading: Program periods; peer review; distribution of subgrants; waivers
children.label: d Program periods; peer review; distribution of subgrants; waivers

children.heading: Grants
children.label: A Grants
children.text: A grant awarded by the Secretary to a State entity under this section shall be for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.heading: Subgrants
children.label: B Subgrants
children.text: A subgrant awarded by a State entity under this section shall be for a period of not more than 5 years, of which an eligible applicant may use not more than 18 months for planning and program design.

children.heading: Peer review
children.label: 2 Peer review
children.text: The Secretary, and each State entity awarding subgrants under this section, shall use a peer-review process to review applications for assistance under this section.

children.label: I
children.text: award not less than 3 grants under this section; and

children.label: II
children.text: fully obligate the first 2 years of funds appropriated for the purpose of awarding grants under this section in the first fiscal year for which such grants are awarded; and

children.label: aa
children.text: whether the State entity is using the grant funds for the agreed upon uses of funds; and

children.label: bb
children.text: whether the full amount of the grant will be needed for the remainder of the grant period; and

children.label: aa
children.text: by using such funds to award grants under this section to other State entities; or

children.label: bb
children.text: in a fiscal year in which the amount of such remaining funds is insufficient to award grants under item (aa), in accordance with subparagraph (B).

children.label: I
children.text: the funds reserved under ; andsection 7221d(a)(2) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: funds otherwise reserved under to carry out national activities under ;section 7221a(b)(2) of this titlesection 7221d of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: to award grants to State entities to carry out the activities described in subsection (b)(1) for the next fiscal year; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to award one year of a grant under subsection (b)(1) to a high-scoring State entity, in an amount at or above the minimum amount the State entity needs to be successful for such year.

children.label: A
children.text: are distributed throughout different areas, including urban, suburban, and rural areas; and

children.label: B
children.text: will assist charter schools representing a variety of educational approaches.

children.label: A
children.text: the waiver is requested in an approved application under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary determines that granting such waiver will promote the purpose of this part.

children.heading: Grants
children.label: 1 Grants
children.text: No State entity may receive a grant under this section for use in a State in which a State entity is currently using a grant received under this section.

children.heading: Subgrants
children.label: 2 Subgrants
children.text: An eligible applicant may not receive more than 1 subgrant under this section for each individual charter school for a 5-year period, unless the eligible applicant demonstrates to the State entity that such individual charter school has at least 3 years of improved educational results for students enrolled in such charter school with respect to the elements described in subparagraphs (A) and (D) of .section 7221i(8) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: support the opening of charter schools through the startup of new charter schools and, if applicable, the replication of high-quality charter schools, and the expansion of high-quality charter schools (including the proposed number of new charter schools to be opened, high-quality charter schools to be opened as a result of the replication of a high-quality charter school, or high-quality charter schools to be expanded under the State entity’s program);

children.label: ii
children.text: inform eligible charter schools, developers, and authorized public chartering agencies of the availability of funds under the program;

children.label: I
children.text: participate in the Federal programs in which the schools and students are eligible to participate;

children.label: II
children.text: receive the commensurate share of Federal funds the schools and students are eligible to receive under such programs; and

children.label: III
children.text: meet the needs of students served under such programs, including students with disabilities and English learners;

children.label: iv
children.text: ensure that authorized public chartering agencies, in collaboration with surrounding local educational agencies where applicable, establish clear plans and procedures to assist students enrolled in a charter school that closes or loses its charter to attend other high-quality schools;

children.label: I
children.text: work with the State educational agency and charter schools in the State to maximize charter school participation in Federal and State programs for which charter schools are eligible; and

children.label: II
children.text: work with the State educational agency to operate the State entity’s program under this section, if applicable;

children.label: I
children.text: is using funds provided under this section for one of the activities described in subsection (b)(1); and

children.label: II
children.text: is prepared to continue to operate charter schools funded under this section in a manner consistent with the eligible applicant’s application for such subgrant once the subgrant funds under this section are no longer available;

children.label: I
children.text: charter schools in local educational agencies with a significant number of schools identified by the State for comprehensive support and improvement under ; andsection 6311(c)(4)(D)(i) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the use of charter schools to improve struggling schools, or to turn around struggling schools;

children.label: I
children.text: recruitment and enrollment practices to promote inclusion of all students, including by eliminating any barriers to enrollment for educationally disadvantaged students (who include foster youth and unaccompanied homeless youth); and

children.label: II
children.text: supporting all students once they are enrolled to promote retention, including by reducing the overuse of discipline practices that remove students from the classroom;

children.label: ix
children.text: share best and promising practices between charter schools and other public schools;

children.label: x
children.text: ensure that charter schools receiving funds under the State entity’s program meet the educational needs of their students, including children with disabilities and English learners;

children.label: xi
children.text: support efforts to increase charter school quality initiatives, including meeting the quality authorizing elements described in paragraph (2)(D);

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a State entity not described in subclause (II), a description of how the State entity will provide oversight of authorizing activity, including how the State will help ensure better authorizing, such as by establishing authorizing standards that may include approving, monitoring, and re-approving or revoking the authority of an authorized public chartering agency based on the performance of the charter schools authorized by such agency in the areas of student achievement, student safety, financial and operational management, and compliance with all applicable statutes and regulations; and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a State entity described in subsection (a)(4), a description of how the State entity will work with the State to support the State’s system of technical assistance and oversight, as described in subclause (I), of the authorizing activity of authorized public chartering agencies; and

children.label: xiii
children.text: work with eligible applicants receiving a subgrant under the State entity’s program to support the opening of new charter schools or charter school models described in clause (i) that are high schools;

children.label: i
children.text: is able to meet and carry out the priorities described in subsection (g)(2);

children.label: ii
children.text: is working to develop or strengthen a cohesive statewide system to support the opening of new charter schools and, if applicable, the replication of high-quality charter schools, and the expansion of high-quality charter schools; and

children.label: iii
children.text: is working to develop or strengthen a cohesive strategy to encourage collaboration between charter schools and local educational agencies on the sharing of best practices;

children.label: I
children.text: a description of the roles and responsibilities of eligible applicants, partner organizations, and charter management organizations, including the administrative and contractual roles and responsibilities of such partners;

children.label: II
children.text: a description of the quality controls agreed to between the eligible applicant and the authorized public chartering agency involved, such as a contract or performance agreement, how a school’s performance in the State’s accountability system and impact on student achievement (which may include student academic growth) will be one of the most important factors for renewal or revocation of the school’s charter, and how the State entity and the authorized public chartering agency involved will reserve the right to revoke or not renew a school’s charter based on financial, structural, or operational factors involving the management of the school;

children.label: III
children.text: a description of how the autonomy and flexibility granted to a charter school is consistent with the definition of a charter school in ;section 7221i of this title

children.label: IV
children.text: a description of how the eligible applicant will solicit and consider input from parents and other members of the community on the implementation and operation of each charter school that will receive funds under the State entity’s program;

children.label: V
children.text: a description of the eligible applicant’s planned activities and expenditures of subgrant funds to support the activities described in subsection (b)(1), and how the eligible applicant will maintain financial sustainability after the end of the subgrant period; and

children.label: VI
children.text: a description of how the eligible applicant will support the use of effective parent, family, and community engagement strategies to operate each charter school that will receive funds under the State entity’s program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of how the State entity will review applications from eligible applicants;

children.label: D
children.text: in the case of a State entity that partners with an outside organization to carry out the State entity’s quality charter school program, in whole or in part, a description of the roles and responsibilities of the partner;

children.label: E
children.text: a description of how the State entity will ensure that each charter school receiving funds under the State entity’s program has considered and planned for the transportation needs of the school’s students;

children.label: F
children.text: a description of how the State in which the State entity is located addresses charter schools in the State’s open meetings and open records laws; and

children.label: G
children.text: a description of how the State entity will support diverse charter school models, including models that serve rural communities.

children.label: A
children.text: each charter school receiving funds through the State entity’s program will have a high degree of autonomy over budget and operations, including autonomy over personnel decisions;

children.label: B
children.text: the State entity will support charter schools in meeting the educational needs of their students, as described in paragraph (1)(A)(x);

children.label: C
children.text: the State entity will ensure that the authorized public chartering agency of any charter school that receives funds under the State entity’s program adequately monitors each charter school under the authority of such agency in recruiting, enrolling, retaining, and meeting the needs of all students, including children with disabilities and English learners;

children.label: D
children.text: the State entity will provide adequate technical assistance to eligible applicants to meet the objectives described in clause (viii) of paragraph (1)(A) and subparagraph (B) of this paragraph;

children.label: i
children.text: assessing annual performance data of the schools, including, as appropriate, graduation rates, student academic growth, and rates of student attrition;

children.label: ii
children.text: reviewing the schools’ independent, annual audits of financial statements prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, and ensuring that any such audits are publically reported; and

children.label: iii
children.text: holding charter schools accountable to the academic, financial, and operational quality controls agreed to between the charter school and the authorized public chartering agency involved, such as through renewal, non-renewal, or revocation of the school’s charter;

children.label: F
children.text: the State entity will work to ensure that charter schools are included with the traditional public schools in decisionmaking about the public school system in the State; and

children.label: i
children.text: information on the educational program;

children.label: ii
children.text: student support services;

children.label: iii
children.text: parent contract requirements (as applicable), including any financial obligations or fees;

children.label: iv
children.text: enrollment criteria (as applicable); and

children.label: v
children.text: annual performance and enrollment data for each of the subgroups of students, as defined in , except that such disaggregation of performance and enrollment data shall not be required in a case in which the number of students in a group is insufficient to yield statically reliable information or the results would reveal personally identifiable information about an individual student.section 6311(c)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a request and justification for waivers of any Federal statutory or regulatory provisions that the State entity believes are necessary for the successful operation of the charter schools that will receive funds under the State entity’s program under this section or, in the case of a State entity defined in subsection (a)(4), a description of how the State entity will work with the State to request such necessary waivers, where applicable; and

children.label: B
children.text: a description of any State or local rules, generally applicable to public schools, that will be waived, or otherwise not apply to such schools.

children.label: A
children.text: the degree of flexibility afforded by the State’s charter school law and how the State entity will work to maximize the flexibility provided to charter schools under such law;

children.label: B
children.text: the ambitiousness of the State entity’s objectives for the quality charter school program carried out under this section;

children.label: C
children.text: the likelihood that the eligible applicants receiving subgrants under the program will meet those objectives and improve educational results for students;

children.label: i
children.text: adequately monitor the eligible applicants receiving subgrants under the State entity’s program;

children.label: ii
children.text: work with the authorized public chartering agencies involved to avoid duplication of work for the charter schools and authorized public chartering agencies; and

children.label: I
children.text: the eligible applicants receiving subgrants under the State entity’s program; and

children.label: II
children.text: quality authorizing efforts in the State; and

children.label: E
children.text: the State entity’s plan to solicit and consider input from parents and other members of the community on the implementation and operation of charter schools in the State.

children.label: i
children.text: allows at least one entity that is not a local educational agency to be an authorized public chartering agency for developers seeking to open a charter school in the State; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a State in which local educational agencies are the only authorized public chartering agencies, the State has an appeals process for the denial of an application for a charter school.

children.label: B
children.text: The State entity is located in a State that ensures equitable financing, as compared to traditional public schools, for charter schools and students in a prompt manner.

children.label: i
children.text: Funding for facilities.

children.label: ii
children.text: Assistance with facilities acquisition.

children.label: iii
children.text: Access to public facilities.

children.label: iv
children.text: The ability to share in bonds or mill levies.

children.label: v
children.text: The right of first refusal to purchase public school buildings.

children.label: vi
children.text: Low- or no-cost leasing privileges.

children.label: D
children.text: The State entity is located in a State that uses best practices from charter schools to help improve struggling schools and local educational agencies.

children.label: E
children.text: The State entity supports charter schools that serve at-risk students through activities such as dropout prevention, dropout recovery, or comprehensive career counseling services.

children.label: F
children.text: The State entity has taken steps to ensure that all authorizing public chartering agencies implement best practices for charter school authorizing.

children.text: providing professional development; and

children.label: i
children.text: Teachers.

children.label: ii

children.label: iii
children.text: Specialized instructional support personnel.
Acquiring supplies, training, equipment (including technology), and educational materials (including developing and acquiring instructional materials).
Carrying out necessary renovations to ensure that a new school building complies with applicable statutes and regulations, and minor facilities repairs (excluding construction).
Providing one-time, startup costs associated with providing transportation to students to and from the charter school.
Carrying out community engagement activities, which may include paying the cost of student and staff recruitment.
Providing for other appropriate, non-sustained costs related to the activities described in subsection (b)(1) when such costs cannot be met from other sources.
The number of students served by each subgrant awarded under this section and, if applicable, the number of new students served during each year of the period of the subgrant.

children.label: A
children.text: how the State entity met the objective of sharing best and promising practices described in subsection (f)(1)(A)(ix) in areas such as instruction, professional development, curricula development, and operations between charter schools and other public schools; and

children.label: B
children.text: if known, the extent to which such practices were adopted and implemented by such other public schools.
The number and amount of subgrants awarded under this section to carry out activities described in each of subparagraphs (A) through (C) of subsection (b)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: how the State entity complied with, and ensured that eligible applicants complied with, the assurances included in the State entity’s application; and

children.label: B
children.text: how the State entity worked with authorized public chartering agencies, and how the agencies worked with the management company or leadership of the schools that received subgrant funds under this section, if applicable.
